validation_velocity.validation_model: 10 custdev interviews + 2 pilot asks + authority-route validation
validation_velocity.binary_pass_signal: At least 2 qualified buyers accept a paid or structured pilot conversation and the required route or authority constraint appears workable.
validation_velocity.binary_kill_signal: No qualified buyer accepts a pilot conversation or the required route constraint blocks delivery economics.
validation_velocity.derivation_basis: Protocol-derived from segment, approval-route complexity, buyer motion, and current validation/citation gaps.
validation_velocity.notes: validation queue severity 4; citation status ready; regulatory route present

demand_pull.derivation_basis: Screenshots confirm local language-training demand and visible French-course terms, but not strong B2B buyer intent by themselves. Added latest SEMrush scorecard sync.
demand_pull.notes: db=LU screenshots show French demand not only on private school domains but also through local institutional and continuing-education routes such as Institut Français Luxembourg and lifelong-learning-style discovery. This remains category evidence, not yet direct employer-paid proof. Latest SEMrush scorecard sync: scope local (fit). Anchor query volume sum 930, max anchor volume 390, capture count 7. Lane keyword count 17 with 5 P1 terms. Latest SEMrush scorecard sync: scope local (fit). Anchor query volume sum 930, max anchor volume 390, capture count 7. Lane keyword count 17 with 5 P1 terms. Top follow-up terms: french classes luxembourg, french courses luxembourg, language courses luxembourg, cours de français luxembourg, cours de langue luxembourg. Cross-border Luxembourg-intent coverage: 3 matching terms in FR/DE seed pack.

regulatory_context.derivation_basis: Derived from confirmed regulatory requirements, approval entities, and current contradiction/risk density in the evidence-backed card.
regulatory_context.notes: 1 confirmed regulatory requirements in current card; 1 high-severity risk flags remain in the current card

semrush_snapshot.serp_feature_notes: Visible SEO demand is centered on local language-school queries and French course intent. Business-specific modifiers should be tested against this base set.
semrush_snapshot.traffic_analytics_notes: The local French lane is visible inside db=LU and has institutional gravity. Institut Français Luxembourg and lifelong-learning-style discovery should sit beside classic private-school competitors.

collapsed_scores.confidence_reason: Market evidence scope is local with scope fit fit. 7 anchor captures contribute to this scorecard sync. Primary evidence database: LU.

market_evidence.derivation_basis: Synced from the latest SEMrush-derived product market scorecard, which combines normalized keyword captures, product-market mapping, and scoped local/cross-border/reference evidence rules.
market_evidence.next_research_step: map lane keywords into product-level commercial notes
market_evidence.notes: Current SEMrush evidence matches the expected scope for this product (local). Evidence database: LU. Captured 7/3 anchor families with total local query volume 930 and max anchor volume 390. Lane rollup contains 17 deduplicated keywords, including 5 P1 terms. Current watchlist domains: inll.lu, lifelong-learning.lu, public.lu, prolingua.lu, vdl.lu. Top follow-up keywords: french classes luxembourg (P2, vol 390), french courses luxembourg (P2, vol 390), language courses luxembourg (P2, vol 320), cours de français luxembourg (P2, vol 140), cours de langue luxembourg (P2, vol 140). Cross-border Luxembourg-intent layer: 3 relevant keywords; top terms french classes luxembourg, french courses luxembourg, language courses luxembourg. Next step: map lane keywords into product-level commercial notes. Independent-operator signal: weak. Examples: Prolingua specialist language (private_school_or_academy), inlingua Luxembourg (private_school_or_academy), INLL (official_or_institutional). Commercial supply exists, but it is led by broad schools and institutions rather than fragmented small operators. Why not stronger: Current evidence shows category demand, but not a clear long tail of niche business-French operators.

independent_operator_examples.name: Prolingua specialist language
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Commercial provider with specialist and tailor-made French offers overlapping with the product.

independent_operator_examples.name: inlingua Luxembourg
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Broad private language school serving the same general business-French lane.

independent_operator_examples.name: INLL
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Institutional provider proving category demand, but not fragmented small-operator supply.

pricing_or_offer_proof: No clean small-operator price proof was captured in the validated evidence set.
independent_operator_summary.social_funnel_signal: Facebook search for French-in-Luxembourg terms collapsed into general Luxembourg and expat groups rather than niche French-learning communities. This supports local newcomer demand, but not a dedicated small-operator funnel for business French.
independent_operator_summary.takeaway: Commercial supply exists, but it is led by broad schools and institutions rather than fragmented small operators.
independent_operator_summary.why_not_stronger: Current evidence shows category demand, but not a clear long tail of niche business-French operators.
